---
title: "Martin Cabrera"
toc: false
---

::: {.profile-header}
::: {.profile-left}
![](../images/equipo/martin.jpg){.profile-photo-large}

::: {.profile-links}
[✉](mailto:martin.cabrera@posgrado.ecologia.edu.mx)
:::
:::

::: {.profile-right}
**PhD Student**  
Instituto de Ecología, A.C. (INECOL)

*Interests:* [Mammals]{.profile-tag} [Bats]{.profile-tag} [Ecomorphology]{.profile-tag} [Macroecology]{.profile-tag} [Macroevolution]{.profile-tag}

I am very interested in the biology of mammals, particularly bats. In my Bachelor's and Master's studies I described the mammal community of Tetela de Ocampo, Puebla, and explored the population genetics of *Sturnira parvidens* throughout its entire geographical range. In my PhD, I describe and analyze the mode of evolution and rates of change of wing morphology and acoustic signals in bats at a global scale, and their impact on macroecological patterns.

### Education

- **PhD** — Instituto de Ecología, A.C. (ongoing)
- **MSc** — Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México (2021)
:::
:::

::: {.profile-back}
[← Back to Team](../equipo.html)
:::
